attack_surface.data_description: Investor (Actor 10) crafts a malicious transaction for Data Flow 302, attempting to alter claim parameters.

security_gap.gap_description: The Airdrop Claim Website (202) or its backend smart contract might have vulnerabilities that allow tampering with claim amounts or eligibility data if input validation is insufficient.

actor_risk.description: Investor (Partner System) attempts to exploit weaknesses in the claim transaction processing.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering could lead to unauthorized withdrawal of $BERA tokens, impacting the airdrop pool and fairness.

assumptions: The claim transaction data (Data Flow 302) might be manipulable client-side before signing, or the smart contract processing the claim has a flaw.
Component 202 relies on client-provided data for claim details that might not be fully re-validated server-side/on-chain.
Actor 10 possesses technical skills to craft and submit tampered transactions.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all claim parameters submitted via Data Flow 302 are rigorously validated on-chain by the smart contract associated with Component 202. Rely on secure, server-side/on-chain sources of truth for eligibility and amounts, not client-provided data.

description: An Investor (Actor 10) attempts to tamper with the data in their airdrop claim transaction (Data Flow 302) submitted to the Berachain Airdrop Claim Website (Component 202). The goal is to modify the claim amount or eligibility criteria to receive more $BERA tokens (Asset 101) than entitled from the airdrop allocation (Asset 115).
